Binary search has a time complexity of O(log n) because the search space is halved at every step, therefore the number of comparisons grows logarithmically. The space complexity is O(1) for the iterative version since no extra memory is used, in other words it works in-place.

Database normalization refers to structuring tables to minimize duplicate data and dependency issues. For instance, splitting an orders table so customer address is not repeated in every row demonstrates 2NF, and further removing derived fields achieves 3NF.
i did not fully understand this topic

Normalization is defined as the process of organizing data to reduce redundancy. For example, a table storing student name and course name repeatedly can be split into separate tables linked by keys. Moving from 1NF to 3NF removes transitive dependencies, such as separating instructor details into their own table.
